meta.name: Semantix
meta.scratchpad: Verification correction: Spec Studio Real Evaluator implementation and mocked tests exist, but final live LLM manual proof is not recorded. ss-llm-006 and ss-llm-008 are reopened/not_started. Fixed fail-closed behavior so --mode llm aborts on evaluatorMode mismatch and SPEC_STUDIO_EVALUATOR=llm without connector reports unavailable instead of silently falling back to probe.

swimlanes.label: Contract
swimlanes.description: Semantix-side packet contracts, fixtures, and validator parity.

swimlanes.label: Evaluator
swimlanes.description: Alignment evaluation, readiness, stable IDs, and user-turn semantics.

swimlanes.label: Integration
swimlanes.description: Phalanx-facing handshake, context requests, and degraded behavior.

swimlanes.label: Proof
swimlanes.description: End-to-end fixtures, reports, and review handoff evidence.

swimlanes.label: Spec Studio JSON Probe
swimlanes.description: Semantix-only manual JSON discussion loop before live Phalanx wiring.

swimlanes.label: Spec Studio Real Evaluator
swimlanes.description: LLM-backed Semantix alignment evaluator behind /spec-studio/evaluate; JSON probe remains explicit test/probe mode only.

tasks.title: Implement Semantix Spec Studio packet contract module
tasks.goal: Add Semantix-side typed contract definitions and validation helpers for the Phalanx Spec Studio alignment packet, context request, context response, and degraded packet shapes.
definition_of_done: Repo-native named exports exist for SemantixAlignmentPacket, SemantixContextRequest, SemantixContextResponse, readiness values ready/needs_user/blocked, existingSystemContext modes new/update/unknown, contextSources, groundedFacts, findings, and nextTurn.

Validation rejects malformed readiness, missing existingSystemContext, ready+unknown mode, ready update without targetSurfaces, ready update without non-change/reuse/compatibility boundaries, grounded facts without evidenceRef, and Staff-owned output fields.
Validation accepts the upstream sample packet semantics for greenfield ready, update ready, ambiguous needs_user, duplicate/replacement blocked, Hoplon-grounded update, and degraded packets.
No Phalanx runtime code is modified from the Semantix repo.
constraints: Do not treat Semantix as Staff or planner authority.
Do not emit Staff design docs, feature puzzles, verify commands, implementation plans, or file-change instructions from the packet.
Keep groundedFacts evidence-backed and separate from assumptions, risks, recommendations, findings, and requirements.
Use targeted tests only.
tasks.comment: Landed packages/stx/src/spec-studio-contracts.js: validators + asserters for SemantixAlignmentPacket / SemantixContextRequest / SemantixContextResponse / contextSources / groundedFacts / findings / nextTurn, plus READINESS, EXISTING_SYSTEM_MODE, FINDING_*, CONTEXT_SOURCE_*, CONTEXT_REQUEST enums, STAFF_OWNED_FIELDS. 6 upstream samples transcribed in tests/fixtures/spec-studio-samples.js. 63 tests pass; full repo green.

context.claude_brief: Read docs/phalanx-spec-studio-integration-contract.md and implement only the Semantix-owned packet contract surface. Keep exports named and add narrow tests.
suggested_tests: npm run test --workspace @semantix/stx -- --runInBand or an equivalent targeted package test command if available

tasks.title: Define Semantix evaluation request and response interface
tasks.goal: Create the Semantix-side evaluation seam Phalanx can call or simulate: raw directive plus prior packet/session state in, alignment packet plus events/contextRequests out.
definition_of_done: SemantixEvaluateRequest supports trigger initial/user_turn/reconsider/context_response/decide_all/skip.
Every non-initial request requires currentPacket, decisions, findings, and contextResponses arrays so stable IDs can be preserved.
SemantixEvaluateResponse returns packet, events, and contextRequests.
The seam can be called in tests without starting a long-running Semantix service.
constraints: Do not hardcode a Project-Phalanx repo path as the only integration mode.
Do not query Hoplon directly from Semantix code.
Do not create a live server in this slice unless explicitly approved.
tasks.comment: Landed packages/stx/src/spec-studio-evaluator.js: EVALUATE_TRIGGER constants for initial/user_turn/reconsider/context_response/decide_all/skip, validateSemantixEvaluateRequest enforcing currentPacket on non-initial triggers and array shape of decisions/findings/contextResponses, validateSemantixEvaluateResponse covering packet+events+contextRequests, and createSemantixEvaluator(impl) seam runnable in tests without a server. 35 targeted tests pass; full repo 123 + 27 green.

context.claude_brief: Implement the evaluator interface as a dormant adapter/helper with tests. Do not start services or wire runtime launch paths.

tasks.title: Preserve stable IDs across Spec Studio turns
tasks.goal: Make Semantix preserve requirement, finding, decision, grounded-fact, and context-request IDs across multi-turn evaluation when Phalanx passes prior session state.
definition_of_done: A follow-up evaluation carries forward unchanged requirement and finding IDs.
Changed requirements/findings are represented through supersession rather than silent same-ID mutation.
Resolved findings remain auditable and are not silently removed.
Tests cover preserved IDs, superseded IDs, dropped IDs rejected/reported, and same-ID mutation rejected/reported.
constraints: Do not rely on stateless one-shot Semantix calls for production Spec Studio sessions.
Decision IDs remain Phalanx-owned; Semantix may preserve or reference them but must not mint canonical audit decision IDs.
tasks.comment: LLM follow-up hardening now repairs omitted IDs, invalid supersession, and same-ID requirement mutations. Mutated REQ-* output becomes a valid supersession pair with a new REQ id; mutated findings preserve the old F-* and mint a new F id. Choice turns record decisions and can resolve linked findings. nextTurn.target is normalized; duplicates still fail.

tasks.title: Implement readiness classifier and update-safety rules
tasks.goal: Teach Semantix to classify ready, needs_user, and blocked for greenfield, update, unknown, duplicate, replacement, and degraded cases using the Spec Studio contract rules.
definition_of_done: Greenfield requests can be ready only when alignedRequirement, scope, must-level acceptance, and obvious exclusions are clear.
Update requests can be ready only with targetSurfaces plus doNotChange/reuse/compatibility boundaries.
Unknown new-vs-update classification returns needs_user with an open question and blocker finding.
Duplicate or replacement of an existing surface without explicit approval returns blocked or needs_user, never ready.
Negative requirements and do-not-change boundaries are first-class requirement facts.
constraints: Missing update target surface means needs_user, not ready.
Do not treat update requests as greenfield when current-system context is missing.
Semantix readiness is advisory; Phalanx remains lock authority.
tasks.comment: Landed packages/stx/src/spec-studio-readiness.js: classifyReadiness handles greenfield (alignedRequirement+scope+must-acceptance+exclusions), update (targetSurfaces + doNotChange/reuse/compatibility), unknown (needs_user + Q-MODE-001 + blocker), duplicate/replacement (blocked/needs_user/explicit). applyReadinessVerdict merges classifier output preserving user findings. promoteNegativeRequirements lifts negatives + doNotChange to first-class facts. docs/plans report added. 24 tests.

tasks.title: Generate Phalanx-brokered context requests
tasks.goal: Emit focused SemantixContextRequest objects when existing-system facts materially affect readiness, instead of inventing facts or querying Hoplon directly.
definition_of_done: Semantix can emit context requests for identify_target_surface, summarize_current_behavior, find_existing_flow, find_reusable_component, find_constraints, collect_hoplon_evidence, and inspect_reference_artifact.
Requests include stable id, sessionId, iteration, purpose, query, requestedFrom, constraints, and reason.
Requests use requestedFrom/allowedSources to ask Phalanx to broker Hoplon/repo/upload/trace access.
Tests prove no direct Semantix-to-Hoplon call path exists in this slice.
constraints: Semantix must not query Hoplon directly.
Do not make unavailable context a fabricated fact.
Only request context when it materially affects readiness or acceptance clarity.
tasks.comment: Landed packages/stx/src/spec-studio-context-requests.js: createContextRequest factory + 7 per-purpose helpers (identify_target_surface, summarize_current_behavior, find_existing_flow, find_reusable_component, find_constraints, collect_hoplon_evidence, inspect_reference_artifact). createContextRequestSequencer mints stable ids. planContextRequests gap-driven planner. Structural tests assert no Hoplon import in stx source or package manifest. 26 tests.

tasks.title: Ingest Phalanx context responses without mixing interpretation into facts
tasks.goal: Consume SemantixContextResponse facts/artifacts from Phalanx and update packets while preserving evidence refs and separating grounded facts from Semantix interpretation.
definition_of_done: Context response facts with evidence refs become groundedFacts with stable FACT IDs, source, text, confidence, and evidenceRef.
Unavailable/empty/error context responses become contextSources with status unavailable or skipped and do not fabricate groundedFacts.
Semantix interpretations from grounded facts go into assumptions, risks, findings, recommendations, or requirement facts, not groundedFacts.
Tests cover successful Hoplon-grounded fact, unavailable context, skipped context, and fact/interpretation separation.
constraints: Grounded facts require evidence refs.
Hoplon is context acquisition only, not spec authority.
User approval and locked artifact remain the desired-behavior authority.
tasks.comment: Landed packages/stx/src/spec-studio-context-ingestion.js: ingestContextResponses maps response status (ok/empty -> used, error -> unavailable, skipped -> skipped), promotes only facts with evidenceRef into groundedFacts, never fabricates facts. recordInterpretationsFromFacts records assumptions/risks/findings/recommendations/requirements with required sourceFactRef and refuses to touch groundedFacts. 17 tests.

tasks.title: Implement user-turn, reconsider, skip, and decide-all semantics
tasks.goal: Support the interactive Spec Studio loop where user answers, free text, reconsiderations, skipped gaps, and delegated Semantix decisions trigger packet re-evaluation.
definition_of_done: Choice and free-text user turns update requirements, findings, decisions, openQuestions, coverage hints, and nextTurn deterministically enough for tests.
Reconsider supersedes prior decisions or requirements instead of rewriting history.
Skip or flag-as-gap preserves an auditable finding or concern.
decided-by-semantix decisions are flagged for human visibility and never treated as canonical Phalanx audit IDs.
constraints: Semantix may propose decision semantics, but Phalanx mints canonical decision IDs.
Findings cannot be silently dismissed.
Do not implement Phalanx persistence in Semantix.
tasks.comment: Landed packages/stx/src/spec-studio-user-turn-loop.js: applyUserChoiceTurn / applyUserFreeTurn / applyReconsiderTurn / applySkipTurn / applyDecideAllTurn. Choice/free/decide-all resolve linked findings + open questions, reconsider supersedes prior decision and reopens findings with reopenReason, skip raises concern (never dismisses blockers), decided-by-semantix entries flagged for human review. sem_dec_ ids stay provisional. Continuity guard now allows reopen with audit reason. 21 tests.

tasks.title: Produce honest degraded/unavailable Semantix packet behavior
tasks.goal: Ensure Semantix partial failure returns needs_user with blocker findings and never silently passes a degraded state into lock or Staff planning.
definition_of_done: Partial Semantix degraded packet uses source semantix, readiness needs_user, a blocker finding, alignmentPct 0 unless prior packet is explicitly stale-safe, and no new ungrounded requirement facts.
Malformed or unavailable model/evaluator output degrades honestly in the Semantix adapter surface.
Tests prove degraded packets validate but are not lockable by downstream contract expectations.
Docs/report state that fully unreachable Semantix fallback envelopes remain Phalanx-owned.
constraints: Do not mark degraded packets ready.
Do not start or modify Phalanx run behavior.
Do not invent facts during degradation.
tasks.comment: Landed packages/stx/src/spec-studio-degraded.js: createDegradedPacket (source=semantix, readiness=needs_user, blocker, alignmentPct=0 unless staleSafe carries prior coverage, no invented facts), isPacketLockable / isDegradedPacket predicates, withDegradationFallback wrapper that catches throws and malformed responses + demotes dishonest ready packets. docs/plans degraded-behavior boundary report added. 20 tests.

tasks.title: Guard against Semantix emitting Staff-owned planning content
tasks.goal: Prevent Semantix output from carrying Staff design docs, feature puzzles, verify commands, implementation plans, or file-change authority inside packets or next-turn bodies.
definition_of_done: Validator/evaluator rejects or reports Staff-owned fields at any packet depth, including nextTurn bodies and requirement extras.
Semantix can recommend clarification, assumptions, risks, findings, and acceptance criteria but not Staff implementation plans.
Tests cover top-level and nested Staff-owned output bleed.
Execution report documents the authority boundary.
constraints: Staff plans only after lock from the locked artifact.
Semantix aligns user intent; it does not execute code or choose verify commands.
Do not weaken packet passthrough so much that Staff-owned fields can hide in nested payloads.
tasks.comment: Landed packages/stx/src/spec-studio-no-staff-authority.js: deep-walking guard catches Staff-owned keys at any depth (requirements, findings, contextSources, groundedFacts, nextTurn.body, options). Case-insensitive fragment match handles mixed_case / snake_case. Free-text fields still allowed. STAFF_OWNED_FIELDS centralized; validateSemantixAlignmentPacket delegates. docs/plans authority-boundary report added. 23 tests.

tasks.title: Build upstream sample fixture and regression matrix
tasks.goal: Create compact Semantix-side fixtures and targeted tests for all sample packet flows and evaluator transitions required by the contract.
definition_of_done: Fixtures cover greenfield ready, update ready, ambiguous needs_user, replacement blocked, Hoplon-grounded update, degraded/unavailable, malformed context, and nested Staff-owned field bleed.
Targeted tests run without a live Phalanx or Hoplon service.
Fixtures stay compact and do not copy unrelated prose from the contract docs.
A report lists what samples were used and what remains out of scope.
constraints: Do not depend on a live Semantix service process in fixture tests.
Do not copy large doc sections into fixtures.
Do not use fixture success as proof of Phalanx lock authority.
tasks.comment: Extended packages/stx/tests/fixtures/spec-studio-samples.js with malformedContextResponseSample, factWithoutEvidenceContextResponseSample, staffOwnedBleedPacket, and 3 evaluateResponseFixtures. Added spec-studio-fixture-matrix.test.js: 38 tests cross-checking validator + classifier + lockability + degraded + staff-guard verdicts per fixture. docs/plans report enumerates in-scope fixtures and out-of-scope concerns. No live services.

tasks.title: Expose a Phalanx capability-handshake adapter
tasks.goal: Provide a small callable Semantix adapter that Phalanx can use to verify Semantix packet generation, malformed-output handling, unavailable behavior, and prior-state preservation.
definition_of_done: Adapter can run in fixture/simulated mode from Phalanx tests without a long-running Semantix service.
Adapter returns packets that pass the Semantix-side and Phalanx-side contract validators.
Unavailable adapter behavior is explicit and degraded, not a thrown opaque failure.
Tests cover successful packet validation, malformed response, unavailable Semantix, and prior-state ID preservation.
constraints: Do not require editing Project-Phalanx from this task.
Do not make Semantix unavailable a terminal Phalanx pipeline failure.
Do not hardcode one machine-specific repo path as the only integration mode.
tasks.comment: Landed packages/stx/src/spec-studio-handshake.js: describeSemantixCapabilities() returns the static handshake summary (contract version, supported triggers/purposes/sources, authority boundaries). createSemantixHandshakeAdapter(options) wraps the evaluator seam with degradation fallback + continuity guard, supports fixture object/function modes, evaluator mode, and unavailable mode (no opaque throws). isHandshakePacketLockable mirrors Phalanx lock criteria. 14 tests.

tasks.title: Prove Semantix-side Spec Studio evaluator flows end to end
tasks.goal: Run a focused Semantix-side proof for new, update, ambiguous, blocked replacement, degraded, Hoplon-grounded context-response, and multi-turn reconsider flows.
definition_of_done: A focused proof suite exercises the evaluator through greenfield ready, update ready, ambiguous needs_user, blocked replacement, degraded, Hoplon-grounded update, reconsider/supersede, and skipped-gap cases.
The proof asserts packet validity, stable IDs, fact/interpretation separation, no Staff-owned output, and correct readiness behavior.
The execution report states exactly which flows are Semantix-side only and which require Phalanx lock/session/handoff work.
Tracker task context records test commands and results for Codex review.
constraints: Do not claim Phalanx UI/session/lock/Staff integration is complete from Semantix-only proof.
Do not start pipeline runs automatically.
Use targeted tests and a concise execution report.
tasks.comment: Landed packages/stx/tests/spec-studio-end-to-end-proof.test.js: 10 tests across 8 flows (greenfield, update, ambiguous, replacement-blocked, degraded, hoplon-grounded ingestion+interpretation, reconsider/supersede, skipped-gap) plus cross-flow regression + authority-boundary assertion. Each packet checked by validator + Staff guard. Commands: node --test packages/stx/tests/... OR npm test. Repo: 333 stx + 27 control-plane green. Phalanx lock/session/Staff out of scope.

tasks.title: Add Spec Studio JSON evaluate endpoint
tasks.goal: Expose a Semantix HTTP endpoint that accepts SemantixEvaluateRequest JSON and returns SemantixEvaluateResponse JSON without requiring Phalanx to run.
definition_of_done: POST /spec-studio/evaluate exists in the Semantix server.
The route uses createSemantixHandshakeAdapter.
Valid initial JSON returns HTTP 200 with a valid SemantixEvaluateResponse.
Invalid request shape returns HTTP 400 with validation details.

context.notes: First gate for JSON-only testing before Phalanx live transport.

tasks.title: Add deterministic Spec Studio discussion evaluator mode
tasks.goal: Provide a deterministic evaluator behind the JSON endpoint so initial -> question -> user answer -> ready can be proven without an LLM or Phalanx.
definition_of_done: Initial trigger returns an ambiguous needs_user packet with nextTurn.
Choice user_turn can return a ready packet.
context_response accepts nested Phalanx context episode shape.
skip, delegate, and reconsider bodies do not crash the endpoint.
Throwing or malformed evaluator behavior degrades honestly to a non-lockable Semantix packet.

context.notes: This is a transport/protocol evaluator, not production semantic reasoning.

tasks.title: Add copy-pasteable manual JSON fixtures
tasks.goal: Create fixture request bodies that can be posted manually to Semantix for every supported discussion turn shape.
definition_of_done: Fixture folder exists under packages/stx/tests/fixtures/spec-studio-manual-json/.
Fixtures include initial, choice-turn, free-turn, skip-turn, delegate-turn, reconsider-turn, context-response-nested, and malformed-request JSON.
Valid fixtures are accepted by automated tests.
Malformed fixture is rejected with HTTP 400.

context.notes: Fixtures should be useful both in tests and by copy-pasting into curl/Postman.

tasks.title: Add Spec Studio manual JSON HTTP regression tests
tasks.goal: Start the Semantix HTTP server in-process and prove the manual JSON fixtures work over HTTP before Phalanx is wired.
definition_of_done: Tests cover initial request without optional arrays.
Tests cover user choice with currentPacket.
Tests cover nested context-response episode.
Tests cover skip, delegate, and reconsider bodies.
Tests cover malformed request returning HTTP 400.
Tests cover evaluator failure returning non-lockable Semantix degraded response.

context.notes: This is the automated proof that manual JSON works before live Phalanx transport.

tasks.title: Add manual JSON probe script
tasks.goal: Provide a command that posts the fixture sequence to a running Semantix server and prints compact readiness/source/iteration output.
definition_of_done: npm script exists for probe:spec-studio-json in @semantix/stx.
Script POSTs initial.json, captures packet, injects it into choice-turn.json, and POSTs the follow-up.
Script exits 0 on valid response sequence.
Script exits nonzero and prints response body on validation failure.

context.notes: This gives the operator a direct Semantix-only smoke command.

tasks.title: Write manual JSON probe operator guide
tasks.goal: Document exactly how to start Semantix, POST the fixtures, and identify a successful JSON-only discussion loop.
definition_of_done: docs/spec-studio-manual-json-probe.md exists.
Guide includes start command, endpoint URL, initial curl, follow-up curl, and success criteria.
Guide explains that Phalanx is not required for this probe.
Guide names the handoff condition for later PHALANX_SEMANTIX_HTTP_URL wiring.

context.notes: This should be short and procedural, not architecture prose.

tasks.title: Run final Semantix-only JSON discussion proof
tasks.goal: Execute the automated and manual JSON probes and record the evidence needed before starting live Phalanx integration.
definition_of_done: node --test packages/stx/tests/spec-studio-manual-json-http.test.js passes.
npm test passes.
Manual probe script reaches a valid response sequence against a running Semantix server.
Tracker context records the exact commands and results.

context.notes: This is the explicit gate before setting PHALANX_SEMANTIX_HTTP_URL in Phalanx.
context.proof_commands:
node --test packages/stx/tests/spec-studio-manual-json-http.test.js → 10/10 pass
npm test --workspace packages/stx → 366/366 pass
node packages/stx/scripts/probe-spec-studio-json.js --url http://127.0.0.1:4402/spec-studio/evaluate → PROBE PASSED (initial→needs_user@iter0, choice→ready@iter1 alignmentPct=100)

tasks.title: Make Spec Studio evaluator backing explicit and fail closed
tasks.goal: Remove ambiguity around /spec-studio/evaluate by making the active evaluator mode visible and preventing the deterministic probe evaluator from being mistaken for production readiness.
definition_of_done: The HTTP route exposes or logs the active evaluator backing as one of probe, llm, or unavailable.
Default behavior is documented and cannot be described as production-ready when backed by the probe evaluator.
Tests assert that probe mode is explicitly labeled in responses, health, docs, or an equivalent observable surface.
Manual JSON docs clearly distinguish transport/probe readiness from LLM-backed readiness.
constraints: Do not remove the probe evaluator; keep it for regression and transport tests.
Do not claim Phalanx integration readiness from probe-mode evidence.

context.reason: Prevents repeating the readiness ambiguity that made a mock transport look like a real evaluator.

tasks.title: Design the LLM-backed Spec Studio evaluator contract
tasks.goal: Define the concrete prompt, input synthesis, output schema, and degradation expectations for a real Semantix evaluator that consumes SemantixEvaluateRequest and emits SemantixEvaluateResponse.
definition_of_done: A repo-local design/spec document names the real evaluator module, injected dependencies, model connector boundary, prompt inputs, and output schema.
The spec covers initial, user_turn, reconsider, skip, decide_all, and context_response triggers.
The spec states how decisions, findings, currentPacket, and contextResponses are summarized into the LLM request.
The spec defines failure behavior: malformed model output degrades honestly and never returns ready.
constraints: No Phalanx runtime dependency in the Semantix evaluator.
No Staff-owned fields may be produced by the evaluator.

context.reason: Locks down what real evaluator means before writing adapter code.

tasks.title: Implement the real LLM-backed Spec Studio evaluator adapter
tasks.goal: Add an evaluator implementation that calls the configured LLM/model connector and returns contract-valid SemantixEvaluateResponse packets instead of canned probe output.
definition_of_done: A new real evaluator module exists and is separate from spec-studio-json-probe-evaluator.js.
The evaluator uses a configured LLM/model connector rather than deterministic canned responses.
The evaluator handles at least initial and user_turn triggers with non-canned packet generation.
The route can be configured to use the LLM evaluator, while probe mode remains explicitly opt-in or explicitly labeled.
All evaluator outputs are validated by the existing Semantix response validator.
constraints: Do not wire Phalanx as part of this task.
Do not silently fall back from LLM mode to probe mode.

context.reason: This is the missing production behavior behind the HTTP transport.

tasks.title: Add robust LLM output repair and degraded handling
tasks.goal: Ensure malformed, partial, or failed LLM evaluator output becomes an honest non-lockable degraded packet instead of a fake ready packet or a hard-to-debug transport failure.
definition_of_done: Malformed model JSON is caught and converted to a needs_user degraded packet with blocker finding.
Ready packets with coverage/findings contradictions are demoted or rejected by tests.
LLM timeout/provider failure never emits readiness ready.
Errors preserve enough diagnostic detail for manual JSON debugging without leaking unrelated runtime noise.
constraints: Never mark degraded output lockable.
Keep Phalanx lock authority semantics unchanged.

context.reason: A real LLM path needs failure semantics before live integration.

tasks.title: Add mocked-LLM regression tests for real iterative discussion
tasks.goal: Prove the real evaluator path performs an iterative discussion loop using mocked LLM responses, not the deterministic probe evaluator.
definition_of_done: Tests run the route in LLM evaluator mode with mocked model responses.
Initial request can produce a non-canned Semantix nextTurn derived from the request.
A follow-up user_turn updates the packet based on prior currentPacket and answer.
Tests fail if the probe evaluator is used accidentally in LLM-mode scenarios.
All new tests pass with the existing workspace test suite.
constraints: Mock the LLM deterministically in tests; do not require network/model access in CI.
Keep probe-mode tests separate and clearly named.

context.reason: This is the first proof that discussion behavior is real rather than canned.

tasks.title: Add manual JSON proof for LLM-backed evaluator mode
tasks.goal: Extend the manual JSON probe so an operator can run the same initial -> follow-up flow against the real LLM evaluator and see evidence that the output is not the canned probe path.
definition_of_done: Manual instructions include how to start Semantix in LLM evaluator mode.
Probe output identifies evaluator backing as llm.
Manual proof requires at least initial -> needs_user and one follow-up turn that changes the packet based on the answer.
Docs state that probe-mode success is insufficient for Phalanx readiness.
A captured proof command/result is recorded in the tracker task context or docs.
constraints: Do not require Phalanx for this proof.
Do not count deterministic probe output as satisfying this task.

context.reason: This is the missing pre-Phalanx proof the user originally needed.
context.verification_status: Complete: live LLM-backed manual proof is recorded in docs/spec-studio-llm-proof.md and the STX suite passes 399/399.
context.completionEvidence: docs/spec-studio-llm-proof.md records LLM-mode proof and 399/399 STX tests passing.
context.contractCorrection: Discrete answer suggestions use nextTurn.body.kind="question" with body.options; outgoing nextTurn.body.kind="choice" is rejected.

tasks.comment: Completed: manual LLM evaluator proof is recorded in docs/spec-studio-llm-proof.md, mode instructions identify evaluatorMode=llm, and proof evidence distinguishes LLM-backed flow from probe mode. Corrected multi-choice prompt shape to Phalanx-compatible question+options.

tasks.title: Gate Phalanx integration on real evaluator proof
tasks.goal: Update docs/tracker handoff so PHALANX_SEMANTIX_HTTP_URL is not treated as ready until the LLM-backed manual JSON proof is complete.
definition_of_done: Phalanx integration docs distinguish transport-ready, probe-ready, LLM-backed ready, and production-ready states.
The next Phalanx task references the LLM-backed manual JSON proof as a prerequisite.
No doc says no Semantix code changes are required unless the route is actually backed by the real evaluator.
Tracker scratchpad records the current readiness state accurately.
constraints: Do not mark live Phalanx integration unblocked from probe-only evidence.

context.reason: Prevents repeating the false readiness handoff.

tasks.title: Run final LLM-backed readiness proof before Phalanx wiring
tasks.goal: Execute and record the final evidence that Semantix can run a real LLM-backed iterative Spec Studio discussion through manual JSON before connecting Phalanx.
definition_of_done: Full test suite passes.
Manual JSON proof passes in LLM evaluator mode.
Proof output shows evaluator backing llm, not probe.
The run reaches a meaningful needs_user/ready/blocked state based on real model output, not a canned packet.
Only after this task is complete may Phalanx live wiring be called ready to start.
constraints: If model access is unavailable, this task remains incomplete.
Do not substitute mocked-LLM tests for this manual proof.

context.reason: This is the explicit go/no-go gate for live Phalanx integration.
context.verification_status: Complete: final LLM-backed readiness proof is recorded and Phalanx-facing nextTurn options now align with the contract.
context.completionEvidence: docs/spec-studio-llm-proof.md gate status and proof/test counts updated.
context.verification: Focused Spec Studio contract/evaluator/Phalanx probe tests passed; full STX suite passed 399/399.
context.phalanxContractAlignment: Semantix nextTurn choice prompts remain question turns with options; option items require id and label.

tasks.comment: Completed: final LLM-backed readiness proof is restored as Phalanx-unblocking evidence after contract review correction. The Phalanx-facing shape now aligns with docs/phalanx-spec-studio-integration-contract.md and tests cover rejecting unsupported outgoing choice turns.
